I joined the team in January 2022 and have since developed strong relationships with the team and the clients I work with and delivered dynamic projects and campaigns, whilst having some fun along the way!

Alongside my role at Intandem, I am also a brand manager at the Yorkshire Mark, a fully trademarked providence mark which champions the amazing Yorkshire based food & drink businesses, and is something I am very proud to be part of. As Brand Manager, I help with the strategic planning and direction of the business, helping to grow the The Yorkshire Mark and increase brand awareness. 

Having gained experience working across both agency and client side, it has taught me the importance of building trust and having open and honest communication to achieve a shared vision and to get outstanding results.

IsTnsnLRQkgNIFvv58AfBNvHZJw1.bio:
Hi everyone, I’m proud to have been leading Simon on the Streets for the past six years. With more than 20 years of experience in the voluntary sector, I am passionate about supporting people experiencing homelessness and social exclusion across our communities.

At Simon on the Streets, we support people who are rough sleeping, sofa surfing, at risk of homelessness, or living in emergency and temporary accommodation. Our work is centred around reaching people at some of the most difficult points in their lives, providing compassionate, person-centred support that helps people feel seen, heard, and valued.

Throughout my career, I have developed extensive experience in homelessness, mental health, drugs and alcohol support, partnership working, service delivery, performance management, and organisational change. I remain deeply committed to ensuring that those most vulnerable in society receive the support, dignity, and opportunities they deserve.

I am incredibly proud to lead an independent charity that works directly within communities to make a meaningful difference to people’s lives every single day.

IsXUgIbhAFNroZp46VzZi6Qmkhr2.bio:
Catherine Thristan, is a highly regarded education professional with a proven track record of delivering results to customers and managing a diverse range of stakeholders. Since joining Leeds University Business School (LUBS) in 2023, she has successfully built mutually beneficial relationships with a multitude of external partners. 

After spending the first eleven years of her career teaching English in Japan – a richly collaborative cultural experience that shaped her values and work ethic – Catherine held a range of positions at London Business School and Imperial College London across education and research, including managing Imperial’s MBA programme suite. 

A move to King’s College London followed, where Catherine was part of a central team responsible for professional education across the university. Progressing to the role of Director of Professional Education at King’s, Catherine successfully expanded the team and its client base, working with companies and organisations globally to design, develop and deliver professional and executive education programmes across all faculties.  
 
In her current role as Director of External Engagement here at LUBS, Catherine continues to collaborate with local businesses, government, third sector, civic and international partners, enabling them to work with the university in multiple ways. 

She has developed a versatile team that nurtures meaningful and collaborative relationships with alumni and external partners, supporting students, research and wider communities to make a positive global impact.

OVgxSab0lPZQjs88M7eOctXTv6z1.bio:
Leonie Millard has been a partner in clinical negligence since 2014 and brought her expertise to the Leeds office of Forbes Solicitors to facilitate the strategic growth of the firm, extending the vision of a regional law firm with a national reach. 

As a seasoned litigator, Leonie has a proven track record in representing Claimants with brain injuries, including those arising from birth, the neonatal period and beyond. She has access to a wealth of tested experts. Her success can be measured by multi-million-pound settlements, access to justice, praise in reviews, understanding the needs of individuals and what motivates families. She is complicit in life-changing law and through skill and attention to detail has reformed hospital practice. She provides hope, guidance, financial and practical futures for individuals and their families. 

Leonie leads a dedicated team of innovative and trusted lawyers at Forbes Solicitors, all committed to making a difference. She works closely with an extensive network of experts facilitating rehabilitation and enabling treatment to improve an inherently difficulty journey for those affected by clinical negligence. She enjoys exploring options, creating solutions and security, changing lives and playing a part in preventing future harm.  

She has a special interest in stillbirth and maternity cases, failure to prevent suicide and cancer cases, Leonie also brings extensive experience across a wide range of medical malpractice area including delays, misdiagnosis and substandard treatment.

R5hZYYoXBScdRgozUumlF0PnQ0E2.bio:
Having many spent years trying to find vegan skin care that worked
with my hyper sensitivity and inflamed skin, I decided to create my own.
Working tirelessly through my early 20’s, testing and formulating, I finally
found the secret that worked: Kalahari melon seed oil. This special hydrating oil reduces inflammation in the skin and can even help those with eczema and psoriasis. I developed our first collection of sensitive skin care in the early part of Year 2000. This was really exciting, but it was only when someone rang to say my products had helped their eczema, that I actually felt a sense of achievement.  As the brand has grown, so has my vision for helping others.  Feeling burnt out, stressed or just tired with every-day life, is extremely difficult to deal with and it’s only when we have a bit of me-time, do we realise, we need to look after ourselves.  It is this vision, that has inspired me to create sleep, mood-enhancing & well-being products, all of which have a sense of purpose - to care for you.  With free trade and natural ingredients, it felt like it was just what the doctor ordered and once I started, I just couldn’t stop.  I’m already formulating new products for next year and the whole process makes me feel relaxed, helps me sleep and gives me good karma".

Hazel x

Hi everyone, my name is Robin Howcroft and I work for Quilter Private Client Advisers as a Financial Planner.  I joined the finance industry in 2014 and have a wealth of experience helping a variety of clients such as high net worth individuals, business owners and corporate companies. 
My role as a Financial Planner means that I help my clients to achieve their long term financial goals, weather that be protecting what matters most, creating a nest egg for retirement or passing wealth onto the next generation in the most tax efficient way possible.
I have completed my Level 4 Diploma in Regulated Financial Planning, in addition to holding qualifications in other specialist areas such as Mortgages, long term care and equity release. I continue to study toward chartered financial planner status.
In my free time I enjoy traveling, seeing family and friends or spending the day in my allotment tending to my  fruits and veggies- weather permitting of course!

I'm Claire, a very proud mum, rescue greyhound owner and crap runner. I also live and breathe writing and communications in both my personal and professional life. 

I've done masses of corporate work, most notably for the NHS, the Department for Work and Pensions, Next and Yorkshire Water, and I'm also a blogger and 40 plus influencer. 

Since creating my blog my40pluslife.me three years ago, I've been a finalist in the Yorkshire Blog Awards, written for Woman and Home, the Yorkshire Times, Yorkshire Businesswoman magazine and others, and been featured in The Sun, The Daily Mirror, Best, Living North and the Yorkshire Evening Post. I also write for HN Magazine on all things lifestyle and host a weekly Instagram live to combat weekend loneliness.  

As a writer, my versatility is my strength. I have decades of experience in both a personal and commercial capacity, so you'll be in safe hands, I promise.

As a writer, my versatility is my strength. I have decades of experience in both a personal and commercial capacity, so you'll be in safe hands, I promise.

mU4MWjbyu1ejfmbnZFKpQXPxF483.bio:
Doodle HR has been created to make HR affordable and available to all companies no matter how small. At Doodle HR, we take a very pragmatic approach and work very closely with our clients, considering ourselves to be an integral part of their management team.

Founded by Nancy Prest in 2013, Doodle HR is based in Harrogate, North Yorkshire. 

Nancy is an experienced HR professional with CIPD graduate qualifications at master’s level, a Diploma in Employment Law, a Chartered Fellowship and over 20 years’ experience in public, private and not-for-profit sectors. 

Nancy is passionate about HR and brings a thorough understanding of regulation and policy development and the importance of compliance for small to medium size businesses. Key HR projects the establishment of HR departments and their operational processes, and the design, delivery and communication of benefits packages as well as include the design and implementation of new HR and Payroll systems, such as SAP, Breathe HR, and People HR.

Nancy works alongside Employment Law Solicitors and continues to update and expand her HR skills to provide clients with expert, up to the minute practical advice, on all day-to-day Human Resources issues as part of the HR Inner Circle with renowned Barrister, and LBC presenter, Daniel Barnett. She also works closely with local recruitment agencies to enable preferential rates for her clients.

In 2015, Nancy was joined in the business by Marie Davies, with Marie becoming Nancy’s business partner in 2019.
mU4MWjbyu1ejfmbnZFKpQXPxF483.Headline: Bespoke HR Solutions

vjcGom5hfyXPkteuDnXyQEd6lZY2.bio:
I'm founder and MD of Snowdrops Consulting – business operations specialists that help founders and entrepreneurs unlock the growth potential in their businesses.

What services do you provide? We specialise in offering personalised operations consulting and implementation services designed to align with your unique business goals and priorities.

Who can benefit from our services? We're here to support start-ups and growing businesses, as they encounter the inevitable operational hurdles along their journey.

What kind of support do you offer? We can assist you with any aspect of your business operations. This includes everything from financial management, handling people and compliance matters, addressing IT needs, managing facilities and mitigating risks. Some common challenges we can help you tackle include improving cash flow and profitability, developing strategic plans and forecasts, meeting client deadlines without compromising quality, scaling your business for growth and optimising resource allocation – both in terms of your team and your finances.

How do you go about your work? Well, our approach begins by really getting under the skin of your business operations. We dig deep to pinpoint any issues, bottlenecks, or areas where things aren't running as smoothly as they could be. Once we've got a handle on that, we craft practical and workable solutions that are tailored just for you. These solutions are designed to bring you measurable improvements.

What sets us apart is that we can either help you put these solutions into action ourselves, or we can guide your internal team through the process. No matter which path you choose, we'll always be here to provide ongoing support, keep an eye on progress, and make sure you're getting the results you need.

Why should you consider us? Well, here's the good news: With over 25 years’ experience in nurturing businesses from solo ventures to 150+ strong teams, especially in the creative and professional services fields, chances are we've overcome the very same challenges you're up against.

But what really makes us stand out is our blend of operations management, internal communications, and project/change management experience. 

We thrive on the thrill of diving into problems, figuring out creative solutions, and discovering more efficient ways of doing things. Plus, we're passionate about helping others and forging strong partnerships to ensure the absolute best results.

Snowdrops Consulting – it’s our mission to help businesses bloom!
vjcGom5hfyXPkteuDnXyQEd6lZY2.Headline: Business Operations Consultancy, Snowdrops Consulting Ltd

wyXnwvWGj3QTYrjOgssErUiWlu92.bio:
After graduating with a First Class Honors in Law, I began my career in Investment
Management in 2004 and am now a Chartered Fellow of the Chartered Institute for
Securities. I work extensively with private clients and intermediaries holding seminars
on the management of private investments as well as advising individuals on their
existing and ongoing investment requirements. I specialise in the management of
family offices, company pension schemes and charitable organisations. I am
honoured to have been shortlisted for 'Wealth Manager of the Year 2022' in
Investment Week’s Women and Investing Awards and do extensive work within
Quilter Cheviot nationally around helping women and their families with their unique
investment journeys.
wyXnwvWGj3QTYrjOgssErUiWlu92.headline: Investment Manager, Quilter Cheviot

xhtf8vkz7RU3rOW8YAHzBjtK7Y52.bio:
Great to be part of this community.  I am the National Sales Director at D3 Office Group, we have all the answers to your office requirements, with our multiple in house processes.  

Thinking stationery?  we have the answers and the solutions to product and cost.

Need uniform?  We have our own in house process with 70 embroidery heads and a new state of the art  printing press with up to 50 units per hour.

Office space not working for you?  the D3 Interiors Team are constantly creating agile work spaces for modern day working, helping employees feeling great about their working environment.  We can change your company's profile and keep your employees onside and eager to come to work.  Everything from single item to complete project led refurbishment , our space planning with full Walk through insight drawings will give you the options you need to make the right decisions.

Personally, I love the arts, theatre, music and film.  I travel frequently and as widely as I can.  Can't stay away from politics and economic news, in fact all current affairs.  Walking in town and country - just love to see everything.  My secret passion would be history, and am a firm believer in Churchill's quote "the further back you look, the further forward you see".

Look forward to getting involved!
xhtf8vkz7RU3rOW8YAHzBjtK7Y52.Headline: D3office.co.uk
